title: New Home Joinery Fitout — Epping
summary: Whole-of-home joinery fitout in Epping — handleless kitchen, butler’s pantry, kitchenette, study, wardrobe, laundry and bathroom all finished in Laminex Sublime Teak and Absolute Oak.
heroAlt: Epping open-plan joinery fitout with kitchen, living area and timber screening
overview: This Epping fitout was scoped as a whole-of-home joinery package from the start. Every room — kitchen, kitchenette, butler’s pantry, study, wardrobe, laundry and bathroom — was designed and built in the same material direction so the new-build shell felt resolved and warm from day one rather than assembled piece by piece.
materials: Laminex Sublime Teak and Absolute Oak throughout — consistent finish across every room
Handleless kitchen with integrated fingerpull profile on all doors and drawers
Study nook with 60mm thick floating shelves and 60mm thick benchtop — substantial, not box-store
Wardrobe, laundry and bathroom joinery all matched to the same timber tone and finish as the kitchen
Soft warm timber used to give the new build a layered, lived-in character from the first day

afterImages.alt: Epping handleless kitchen in Laminex Sublime Teak with stone benchtop and integrated appliances
afterImages.caption: Handleless kitchen with fingerpull profile — Laminex Sublime Teak cabinetry, stone benchtop and integrated appliances running the full length of the wall.

afterImages.alt: Epping kitchen island with stone top and timber base cabinetry
afterImages.caption: Island bench with stone top and matching timber base — the centrepiece of the open-plan zone with the pantry and oven wall visible behind.

afterImages.alt: Epping kitchen oven tower and full-height cabinetry in Sublime Teak
afterImages.caption: Full-height oven tower and tall cabinetry in Sublime Teak — clean, handleless lines from floor to ceiling.

afterImages.alt: Epping kitchen detail showing fingerpull profile on lower drawers
afterImages.caption: Fingerpull detail on the lower drawers — the handleless profile keeps the cabinetry clean while staying practical every day.

afterImages.alt: Epping open-plan kitchen and dining zone with herringbone floor and full-width glazing
afterImages.caption: Kitchen and dining zone with herringbone flooring and full-width glazing — the joinery sets the tone for the whole open-plan space.

afterImages.alt: Epping kitchen sink and splashback detail with overhead cabinetry
afterImages.caption: Sink and splashback run with overhead cabinetry in Sublime Teak — functional layout with clean lines throughout.

afterImages.alt: Epping kitchen corner view showing integrated rangehood and cabinetry layout
afterImages.caption: Corner view showing integrated rangehood and the full cabinetry run — everything flush and integrated within the same panel line.

afterImages.alt: Epping kitchen overview with stone island bench, Sublime Teak cabinetry and living zone beyond
afterImages.caption: Overview of the kitchen with stone island, timber cabinetry and the living zone beyond — the whole floor plan connected through one material direction.

afterImages.alt: Epping kitchen lower cabinetry drawers and base units in handleless Sublime Teak
afterImages.caption: Lower cabinetry with deep drawers and handleless fronts — the fingerpull runs consistently across all base units.

afterImages.alt: Epping kitchen wall cabinetry and benchtop with stone and timber detail
afterImages.caption: Wall cabinetry and benchtop detail — stone and timber working together across the full run of the kitchen.

afterImages.alt: Epping butler's pantry with open shelving, stone splashback nook and bar storage
afterImages.caption: Butler’s pantry with adjustable open shelving, a stone splashback nook and dedicated bar storage — keeps the main kitchen clear while giving everything a proper home.

afterImages.alt: Epping kitchenette with full-height timber cabinetry, oven and fluted folding door
afterImages.caption: Secondary kitchenette with full-height Sublime Teak cabinetry and integrated oven — separated from the entry by a fluted folding door and finished to the same standard as the main kitchen.

afterImages.alt: Epping secondary kitchen zone with full timber cabinetry, sink and small dining setting
afterImages.caption: Secondary kitchen zone with full-height cabinetry, integrated sink and a small dining setting — self-contained and carrying the same finish as the main kitchen.

afterImages.alt: Epping study nook with 60mm floating shelves and built-in timber desk in Sublime Teak
afterImages.caption: Study nook with 60mm thick floating shelves and a 60mm thick benchtop in Sublime Teak — the extra thickness makes a real difference to how the joinery reads in the space.

afterImages.alt: Epping study built-in desk and shelving with centre drawers
afterImages.caption: Built-in desk with centre drawers and open shelving above — a proper work zone sized to fit the room without overwhelming it.

afterImages.alt: Epping study joinery detail showing floating shelf thickness and timber grain
afterImages.caption: Shelf detail — the 60mm thickness gives the floating shelves a presence that standard 16mm board shelving simply doesn’t have.

afterImages.alt: Epping study nook full view with desk, shelves and living zone
afterImages.caption: Full view of the study nook sitting within the open-plan living zone — contained, useful and matched to the rest of the home.

afterImages.alt: Epping study nook cabinetry and lounge area with Sublime Teak joinery
afterImages.caption: The study nook and lounge zone in one frame — the joinery anchors the space without carving the floor plan into separate rooms.

afterImages.alt: Epping built-in wardrobe with full-height Sublime Teak doors and internal fittings
afterImages.caption: Built-in wardrobe with full-height Sublime Teak doors — same handleless profile as the kitchen, same material direction carried through to the bedroom.

afterImages.alt: Epping wardrobe internal view with hanging rail, shelves and drawers
afterImages.caption: Internal wardrobe layout with hanging rail, adjustable shelves and drawers — fitted out properly rather than left as an empty carcass.

afterImages.alt: Epping wardrobe door detail in Laminex Sublime Teak with fingerpull profile
afterImages.caption: Door detail — Sublime Teak with fingerpull profile, matching the kitchen cabinetry on the other side of the home.

afterImages.alt: Epping bedroom with built-in wardrobe and matching timber joinery
afterImages.caption: Bedroom view with the built-in wardrobe in position — the timber finish ties the bedroom back to the kitchen and living zone.

afterImages.alt: Epping wardrobe overview showing full run of cabinetry across bedroom wall
afterImages.caption: Full wardrobe run across the bedroom wall — measured to fit the room and designed to feel built-in rather than added on.

afterImages.alt: Epping laundry with Sublime Teak cabinetry, stone benchtop and integrated appliances
afterImages.caption: Laundry with Sublime Teak overhead and base cabinetry, stone benchtop and integrated washer and dryer — the same finish as the kitchen, not an afterthought.

afterImages.alt: Epping laundry joinery detail with upper cabinetry and utility storage
afterImages.caption: Laundry upper cabinetry detail — practical storage with the same handleless profile and timber finish running through from the rest of the home.

afterImages.alt: Epping bathroom with freestanding bath, reeded timber vanity, vessel sink and brass tapware
afterImages.caption: Main bathroom with freestanding bath, reeded floating vanity, vessel sink, brass tapware and backlit round mirror — the timber finish carried through from the kitchen at the same level of quality.

afterImages.alt: Epping bathroom vanity and mirror detail with Sublime Teak reeded front and brass fixtures
afterImages.caption: Vanity detail — reeded Sublime Teak front with vessel sink and brass tapware. The reeding adds texture without breaking the material consistency of the home.

beforeImages.alt: Epping new-build shell before any joinery installation — bare framing and plasterboard
beforeImages.caption: Bare new-build shell before installation started — raw framing and plasterboard, nothing fitted.

beforeImages.alt: Epping kitchen carcasses being set during early installation
beforeImages.caption: Early installation stage — carcasses being set before doors, benchtops or finishes were fitted.

beforeImages.alt: Epping kitchen cabinetry structure mid-installation showing carcass layout
beforeImages.caption: Mid-installation — the carcass structure in place with the layout locked in before fronts and finishes went on.

beforeImages.alt: Epping kitchen zone during early installation — first carcasses being set on bare shell
beforeImages.caption: First carcasses going in — the whole kitchen and living zone mapped out before a single door or benchtop was fitted.

beforeImages.alt: Epping kitchen upper and lower carcasses being set out during installation
beforeImages.caption: Upper and lower carcasses being set out — the layout taking shape before doors and fronts went on.

beforeImages.alt: Epping kitchen carcasses installed with drawers and tall units in position before fit-off
beforeImages.caption: Carcasses, drawer runners and tall units all in position — the structure of the joinery fully set before the final fronts and finishes were fitted.

processImages.alt: Epping joinery installation in progress — cabinetry being set and levelled on site
processImages.caption: Installation in progress — cabinetry being set and levelled before scribing and fit-off.

processImages.alt: Epping kitchen installation mid-stage with carcasses and door fronts being fitted
processImages.caption: Mid-install stage — door fronts going on as the kitchen takes its final shape.

processImages.alt: Epping joinery installation showing cabinetry run being built out across the zone
processImages.caption: The cabinetry run building out across the zone — this is what the process looks like before the finished product.

processImages.alt: Epping installation detail showing joinery structure and panel work in progress
processImages.caption: Panel work and structure in progress — the detail that goes into the build before any door goes on.

spaces.title: Kitchen
spaces.text: Handleless kitchen with fingerpull profile, Laminex Sublime Teak cabinetry, stone benchtop and integrated appliances. Designed as the centrepiece of the open-plan zone.
spaces.alt: Epping handleless kitchen in Laminex Sublime Teak

spaces.title: Butler’s pantry
spaces.text: The butler’s pantry runs off the main kitchen with adjustable open shelving, a stone splashback nook and dedicated bar storage — keeps the main kitchen clear and gives everything a proper home.
spaces.alt: Epping butler's pantry with open shelving and stone nook

spaces.title: Kitchenette
spaces.text: A secondary kitchenette with full-height Sublime Teak cabinetry, integrated oven and sink — separated from the entry by a fluted folding door and finished to the same standard as the main kitchen.
spaces.alt: Epping secondary kitchenette with full-height timber cabinetry

spaces.title: Study nook
spaces.text: 60mm thick floating shelves and a 60mm thick benchtop — a proper built-in work zone that sits within the living space without taking over a room.
spaces.alt: Epping study nook with 60mm floating shelves and timber desk

spaces.title: Wardrobe
spaces.text: Full-height built-in wardrobe with the same handleless Sublime Teak profile as the kitchen — fitted internally with hanging rail, shelves and drawers.
spaces.alt: Epping built-in wardrobe in Laminex Sublime Teak

spaces.title: Laundry
spaces.text: Laundry cabinetry in Sublime Teak with stone benchtop and integrated appliances — the same finish as the kitchen, not an afterthought.
spaces.alt: Epping laundry with Sublime Teak cabinetry and stone benchtop

spaces.title: Bathroom vanity
spaces.text: Reeded Sublime Teak floating vanity with vessel sink, brass tapware and backlit mirror. The material direction from the kitchen carried through at the same level of quality.
spaces.alt: Epping bathroom with reeded timber vanity, freestanding bath and brass tapware

outcome: The completed home feels architectural and settled because every room was given the same material direction and the same level of finish. No room looks like a cost-cut version of another.
ctaText: Building or fitting out a new home in Sydney? We can scope the kitchen, pantry, study, wardrobes and laundry as one package so the whole home feels resolved from day one.
